name: From Mondéjar to Castillo de Almoguera — circular along the bed of the Tajo River
name_original: De Mondéjaral Castillo de Almoguera — circular por el cauze del Río Tajo
region_intro: Road cycling around Almoguera offers diverse landscapes for exploration. The region, situated in the province of Guadalajara, Spain, features picturesque river valleys, including the Tajuña and Tajo Rivers, characterized by lush meadows and riparian vegetation. Road cyclists can navigate varied terrain, encompassing Mediterranean forests, dehesas, and open fields. This area provides a mix of well-established routes suitable for different cycling preferences and skill levels. Best road cycling routes around Almoguera The most popular road cycling route is From Mondéjar to Castillo de Almoguera — circular along the bed of the Tajo River, a 52.1 miles (83.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 42 minutes to complete. This route follows the Tajo River, offering views of the surrounding landscape. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Roadbike loop from Orusco de Tajuña, a moderate 26.5 miles (42.6 km) path. This route explores the Tajuña River valley, known for its scenic views. Local road cyclists also love the Pine Forest Trail – Monument to the Eyes loop from Mazuecos, a 47.9 miles (77.0 km) trail leading through varied landscapes of fields and forests, often completed in about 3 hours 32 minutes. Road cycling around Almoguera is defined by river valleys, Mediterranean forests, and open fields. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Almoguera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from 4 reviews. More than 550 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Almoguera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Almoguera?
A: There are over 50 road cycling routes in the Almoguera region. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 6 easy, 33 moderate, and 11 difficult options available for exploration.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Almoguera?
A: Road cycling in Almoguera offers a diverse landscape. You'll encounter picturesque river valleys, notably along the Tajuña River, characterized by lush meadows and riparian vegetation. Routes also traverse Mediterranean forests, dehesas, and open fields, providing a varied cycling experience.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes in Almoguera?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Almoguera are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38269760" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roadbike loop from Estremera&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 63.3 km route that allows you to start and finish in the same location, offering a convenient cycling experience.

Q: What natural features can I see while road cycling around Almoguera?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. Along your rides, you can discover significant natural monuments such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3255898" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Encina de la Pica&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3455613" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Arca&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7148914" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Isidro Spring and Singular Tree of Villar del Olmo&lt;/a&gt;. These highlights offer scenic stops amidst the Mediterranean forests and river valleys.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, several routes pass by interesting landmarks. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3219959" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chapel of the Holy Children&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1749974" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picturesque bridge&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3825118" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Junction of the Tajuña Greenway and the Tren de los 40 Días Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, showcasing the region's cycling infrastructure.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Almoguera?
A: The road cycling routes around Almoguera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from 4 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, which includes river valleys, Mediterranean forests, and open fields, as well as the well-established greenways that make for enjoyable rides.

Q: What is a good route for experienced road cyclists looking for a challenge?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40308683" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument to the Eyes – Picturesque bridge loop from Orusco de Tajuña&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 88.4 km route with significant elevation gain, offering a demanding yet rewarding experience through varied landscapes.

Q: Are there any routes that follow the Tajuña River valley?
A: Absolutely. The Tajuña River valley is a prominent feature of the region's cycling landscape. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10383371" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roadbike loop from Orusco de Tajuña&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 42.6 km path that specifically explores this scenic river valley.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling in Almoguera?
A: While specific weather data isn't provided, regions like Almoguera in central Spain typically offer pleasant cycling conditions during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, and winters may bring cooler temperatures, so checking local forecasts is always recommended.

Q: Are there options for longer road cycling tours in the area?
A: Yes, Almoguera offers several longer routes for those looking to spend more time on the road. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014700674/from-mondejar-to-castillo-de-almoguera-circular-along-the-bed-of-the-tajo-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Mondéjar to Castillo de Almoguera — circular along the bed of the Tajo River&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 83.9 km route, typically taking around 3 hours and 42 minutes to complete, following the Tajo River.

Q: What kind of natural environment defines the road cycling experience in Almoguera?
A: The road cycling experience in Almoguera is defined by its diverse natural environment, including lush river valleys like the Tajuña and Tajo, extensive Mediterranean forests, and open dehesas. This mix provides constantly changing scenery, from shaded riparian paths to open, sun-drenched fields.
tips: Mondéjar has the guarantee of the Designation of Origin of its wines (in 2016, the harvest was three million kilos of grapes). Cultural things that may interest you: The parish church of Santa María Magdalena, the hermitage of San Sebastián and the convent of San Antonio (although in ruins).
Hermitage that is located at the foot of the road and that welcomes the population of Brea del Tajo. Small but pretty!
The castle of Vállaga, today in ruins, is a fortification of Arab origin built in the 12th century that was later used as a wine cellar and known as the Cuevas del Marqués.

The castle of Almoguera dates from the 15th century, although it was built on the ruins of an old Arab fortification. Little remains of this medieval construction, as the current castle is a reconstruction from the end of the 20th century.
